summary.htmlContent:
Quality Council Meeting Summary

Top 3 Problems Identified

  • High number of overdue effectiveness checks and long-term actions.
  • Repeated deviations due to human factors and knowledge gaps in processes.
  • Resource constraints and audit risks related to M building and temporary instructions.

Key actions

  • Follow up on overdue long-term actions and effectiveness checks.
  • Formalize operator process knowledge sessions and address knowledge gaps.
  • Escalate need for long-term solution for temporary instructions to central quality.
  • Track QIP actions for OSD for 2026 in quality council.
  • Address ongoing DI deviations and review data integrity issues.
  • Plan periodic reviews 3 months in advance to avoid delays.

Decisions Made

  • Keep high number of overdue effectiveness checks as a focus area.
  • Maintain escalation for long-term solution for temporary instructions.
  • Continue planning for periodic reviews and formalize knowledge sessions.
  • Proceed with change control for commissioning to manage audit risks.

Escalations Required

  • Escalate TI (Temporary Instruction) issue to Psychology Council for alternative solutions.
  • Escalate resource constraints for M building commissioning if it becomes active.
  • Escalate long-term solution for temporary instructions to central quality.

Suggestions for Process Confirmations for Next Month

  • Confirm progress on overdue long-term actions and effectiveness checks.
  • Validate implementation of knowledge sessions and training plans.
  • Review status of escalated TI solution and resource allocation for M building.
  • Ensure QIP actions tracking mechanism is in place for 2026.

Communications to cascade to team

  • Emphasize importance of adherence to processes to avoid deviations.
  • Share updates on overdue actions and plans for resolution.
  • Communicate upcoming periodic reviews and training requirements.
  • Inform about escalations and their implications for operations.
